title: Dactylorchis hoeppneri
intro: Dactylorchis hoeppneri, also known as the orchid of the Alps, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis hoeppneri include glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dactylorchis hoeppneri is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or extract, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Dactylorchis hoeppneri is an orchid species that has undergone reclassification. It was previously classified as part of the genus Dactylorhiza but is now sometimes placed under the genus Coeloglossum. However, its classification can vary depending on the source. Some sources consider it a synonym of Coeloglossum viride, while others treat it as a distinct species. Further research is needed to determine its current taxonomic classification.
